lede: A look at December 25 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Isaac Newton and Humphrey Bogart, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Mikhail Gorbachev
events.desc: Mikhail Gorbachev resigns as President of the Soviet Union (the union itself is dissolved the next day). Ukraine's referendum is finalized and Ukraine officially leaves the Soviet Union.

events.title: Romanian revolution
events.desc: Romanian Revolution: Deposed President of Romania Nicolae Ceaușescu and his wife, Elena, are condemned to death and executed after a summary trial.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Battle of Hong Kong ends, beginning the Japanese occupation of Hong Kong.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: A series of unofficial truces occur across the Western Front to celebrate Christmas.

events.title: Richard Wagner
events.desc: Wagner's Siegfried Idyll is first performed.

events.title: Ephraim McDowell
events.desc: Dr. Ephraim McDowell performs the first ovariotomy, removing a 22-pound tumor.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of the Christmas cantata Unser Mund sei voll Lachens, BWV 110, making laughter audible in singing.

events.title: Baldwin I of Jerusalem
events.desc: Baldwin of Boulogne is crowned the first King of Jerusalem in the Church of the Nativity in Bethlehem.

events.title: Henry III, Holy Roman Emperor
events.desc: Henry III is crowned Holy Roman Emperor by Pope Clement II.

events.title: AD 36
events.desc: Forces of Emperor Guangwu of the Eastern Han, under the command of Wu Han, conquer the separatist Chengjia empire, reuniting China.

deaths.text: Tristan Tzara, Romanian-French poet, playwright, painter, and critic
Barbara von Krüdener, German mystic and author
Leo V the Armenian, Byzantine emperor from 813 to 820

observances.text: Christmas Day, Christian festival commemorating the birth of Jesus. (Internationally observed)
Christian feast day: Anastasia of Sirmium (Catholic Church)
Children's Day (Cameroon, Central African Republic, Chad, Equatorial Guinea, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Gabon, Republic of Congo)
